chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ الْحُرِّ يَقْتُلُ مَمْلُوكَ غَيْرِهِ أَوْ يَجْرَحُهُ وَ الْمَمْلُوكِ يَقْتُلُ الْحُرَّ أَوْ يَجْرَحُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – The free man kills a slave of someone else, or injures him, and the slave kills the free or injures him

text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي عَبْدٍ فَقَأَ عَيْنَ حُرٍّ وَ عَلَى الْعَبْدِ دَيْنٌ إِنَّ عَلَى الْعَبْدِ حَدّاً لِلْمَفْقُوءِ عَيْنُهُ وَ يَبْطُلُ دَيْنُ الْغُرَمَاءِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineenasws said regarding a slave who blinded an eye of a free, and upon the slave was a debt: ‘Upon the slave is a legal penalty (Hadd) for the blinding of his eye, and it would invalidate the debt of the creditors’.
